fr:title.text: Coherent remainder as a path object

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Coherent remainder as a path object

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let f\colon A\to B be a function equipped with a section \sigma \equiv \mathopen {}\left \lparen g\colon B\to A,\epsilon \colon f\circ g = 1_{B}\right \rparen \mathclose {} . We can equip the coherent remainder of \sigma with the structure of a path object as follows:

fr:ol.fr:li: We equip g\circ f\sim 1_{A} with the following dependent product of intrinsic path object structures: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x \right \rparen \mathclose {}

For each \eta \colon g\circ f\sim 1_{A}, we likewise equip \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta \sim \epsilon \circ f with the following dependent product of intrinsic path object structures: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {}

fr:li.fr:p: We exhibit the latter as a right-cloven family of path objects over the former with the cleaving that for \eta _0,\eta _1\colon g\circ f\sim 1_{A} and \eta _{01}\colon \eta _0 \sim \eta _1 defines \eta _{01}^*\colon \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _1\sim \epsilon \circ f \to \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _0\sim \epsilon \circ f as follows:

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: With this cleaving in mind, finally define \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma to be the following contravariant sum of path objects:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma :\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {}

fr:title.text: Coherence of different notions of equivalence › coherence of inverses

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Coherence of different notions of equivalence › coherence of inverses

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen g,\epsilon \right \rparen \mathclose {} :\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta \sim \epsilon \circ f
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: For any f\colon A\to B, it is evident that the type of inverses to f is (up to reassociation) precisely the dependent sum \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}.

fr:title.text: Coherent remainder as a path object

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Coherent remainder as a path object

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let f\colon A\to B be a function equipped with a section \sigma \equiv \mathopen {}\left \lparen g\colon B\to A,\epsilon \colon f\circ g = 1_{B}\right \rparen \mathclose {} . We can equip the coherent remainder of \sigma with the structure of a path object as follows:

fr:ol.fr:li: We equip g\circ f\sim 1_{A} with the following dependent product of intrinsic path object structures: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x \right \rparen \mathclose {}

For each \eta \colon g\circ f\sim 1_{A}, we likewise equip \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta \sim \epsilon \circ f with the following dependent product of intrinsic path object structures: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {}

fr:li.fr:p: We exhibit the latter as a right-cloven family of path objects over the former with the cleaving that for \eta _0,\eta _1\colon g\circ f\sim 1_{A} and \eta _{01}\colon \eta _0 \sim \eta _1 defines \eta _{01}^*\colon \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _1\sim \epsilon \circ f \to \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _0\sim \epsilon \circ f as follows:

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: With this cleaving in mind, finally define \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma to be the following contravariant sum of path objects:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma :\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} =x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathord {\vartriangle } \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {act}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {}

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \sigma \equiv \mathopen {}\left \lparen g\colon B\to A,\epsilon \colon f\circ g =1_{B}\right \rparen \mathclose {} be a section of a function f\colon A\to B. Then the coherent remainder of \sigma is a proposition.
By , we may argue that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of a proposition. We let P be the following type defined using path object structure of fibers given in :
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} P &:\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} ,\epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} \cong _{\mathsf {fiber}\,f\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,\mathsf {refl}\right \rparen \mathclose {} \\ P &\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{f}p\cdot \mathsf {refl} \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: It is not difficult to show using that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of P; we will not need the further fact that it is equivalent to P.

Therefore, it remains to show that P is a proposition. Without loss of generality, we may assume that we have some h: P. By , we may fix x:A and prove that \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} ,\epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} \cong _{\mathsf {fiber}\,f\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,\mathsf {refl}\right \rparen \mathclose {} is a proposition. Of course, this is a retract of the identification type \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} ,\epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} =_{\mathsf {fiber}\,f\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,\mathsf {refl}\right \rparen \mathclose {} and so by it is enough to prove that the latter is a proposition. In fact, we will deduce this via by observing that the fiber of f at fx is contractible: using our assumption h:P and the distributivity of products over sums, we may construct a retraction of f exhibiting f as biinvertible and thus by contractible.
For any function f\colon A\to B, the type of inverses to f is a proposition.

fr:title.text: Being an embedding is a proposition

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Being an embedding is a proposition

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: That a given function f\colon A \to B is an embedding is a proposition.
By and .

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \sigma \equiv \mathopen {}\left \lparen g\colon B\to A,\epsilon \colon f\circ g =1_{B}\right \rparen \mathclose {} be a section of a function f\colon A\to B. Then the coherent remainder of \sigma is a proposition.
By , we may argue that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of a proposition. We let P be the following type defined using path object structure of fibers given in :
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} P &:\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} ,\epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} \cong _{\mathsf {fiber}\,f\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,\mathsf {refl}\right \rparen \mathclose {} \\ P &\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} = x\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} = \mathsf {act}_{f}p\cdot \mathsf {refl} \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: It is not difficult to show using that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of P; we will not need the further fact that it is equivalent to P.

Therefore, it remains to show that P is a proposition. Without loss of generality, we may assume that we have some h: P. By , we may fix x:A and prove that \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} ,\epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} \cong _{\mathsf {fiber}\,f\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,\mathsf {refl}\right \rparen \mathclose {} is a proposition. Of course, this is a retract of the identification type \mathopen {}\left \lparen g \mathopen {}\left \lparen f x\right \rparen \mathclose {} ,\epsilon \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} =_{\mathsf {fiber}\,f\, \mathopen {}\left \lparen fx\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,\mathsf {refl}\right \rparen \mathclose {} and so by it is enough to prove that the latter is a proposition. In fact, we will deduce this via by observing that the fiber of f at fx is contractible: using our assumption h:P and the distributivity of products over sums, we may construct a retraction of f exhibiting f as biinvertible and thus by contractible.

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f} \mathopen {}\left \lparen g,\epsilon \right \rparen \mathclose {} :\equiv \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \eta : g\circ f \sim 1_{A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta \sim \epsilon \circ f
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: For any f\colon A\to B, it is evident that the type of inverses to f is (up to reassociation) precisely the dependent sum \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen \mathsf {section}\,f\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}\mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}.

fr:title.text: Contravariant sum of right-cloven families of path objects

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Contravariant sum of right-cloven families of path objects

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {B}\colon \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathsf {PathObj} be an right-cloven family of path objects over a path object \mathcal {A}. We define the contravariant sum of the family \mathcal {B}, written \mathchoice {\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:\mathcal {A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:\mathcal {A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:\mathcal {A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:\mathcal {A}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\mathcal {B} x}, to be the total path object of the displayed path object associated to \mathcal {B} as in . In particular, we have defined \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,u\right \rparen \mathclose {} \approx _{\mathchoice {\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \int _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x:A\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}Bx} \mathopen {}\left \lparen y,v\right \rparen \mathclose {} to be \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : x\approx _{\mathcal {A}} y\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : x\approx _{\mathcal {A}} y\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : x\approx _{\mathcal {A}} y\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen p : x\approx _{\mathcal {A}} y\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} u \approx _{\mathcal {B} x} p^*v.

fr:title.text: Right cleaving of a family of path objects

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Right cleaving of a family of path objects

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {A} be a path object and let \mathcal {B}\colon \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathsf {PathObj} be a family of path objects. A right cleaving of \mathcal {B} over \mathcal {A} is defined by the following data:

fr:ol.fr:li: a pullback operation assigning to each edge p\colon x\approx _{\mathcal {A}} y a map p^* \colon \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {B} y\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {B} x\right \rvert \mathclose {} ;

a family \Phi _{\mathcal {B}} \colon \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} ,b: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {B} x\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} ,b: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {B} x\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} ,b: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {B} x\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} ,b: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {B} x\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }} b \approx _{\mathcal {B} x} \mathsf {rx}_{\mathcal {A}}{x}^*b witnessing the unit law that pullback along the reflexivity datum \mathsf {rx}_{\mathcal {A}}{x}\colon x\approx _{\mathcal {A}} x is the identity function.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The product of a family \mathcal {B}\colon A \to \mathsf {PathObj} path objects is a path object.
Univalence follows from the closure of families of contractible types under dependent products () via the distributivity of \prod over \sum .

A reflexive graph \mathcal {A} is called univalent when for all x: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} the type \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen y: \mathopen {}\left \lvert \mathcal {A}\right \rvert \mathclose {} \right \rparen \mathclose {} }} x\approx _{\mathcal {A}} y is a proposition, or (equivalently), is contractible with centre \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,\mathsf {rx}_{\mathcal {A}}{x}\right \rparen \mathclose {} . We shall refer to a univalent reflexive graph as a path object.
We will write \mathsf {PathObj} for the (large) type of path objects. In our notation, we will at times silently coerce a given path object to its underlying type of vertices.

fr:title.text: The intrinsic path object on a type

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The intrinsic path object on a type

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The discrete reflexive graph on a type A is a path object, which we shall refer to as the intrinsic path object on A.
Univalence follows immediately by identification induction.
